not_publishable_reason: Source-art alignment proof only; no Short plate, no motion proof, no audio render, and requires human review before trailer promotion.

variants.label: variant_01_broad_left_side_prompt
variants.prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept.
Asset type: 16:9 YouTube channel trailer source plate, text-free base image.
Input image role: the attached image is a composition and Paper Architecture style reference only. Do not edit it, trace it, reuse it, or copy its pixels.

Primary request: regenerate the Challenger Paper Architecture source plate from scratch from a new camera angle. Match the attached reference's premium ink-lit Paper Architecture material language, left/center-left staging, dark ink-blue negative-space layout, museum-model lighting, cream folded paper forms, foam-core terraces, vellum/gouache surfaces, scored seams, and soft lavender shadows, but change the shuttle/pad camera so the visible 3/4 view is the shuttle's left-hand side.

Required exact direction: regenerate the scene from a new camera angle; do not mirror or flip the reference. Show the shuttle's left-hand side in a 3/4 view. Use physically plausible lighting and shadows for the new angle.

Scene/backdrop: deep clean ink-blue studio field. The Space Shuttle Challenger launch stack and launch pad sit left/center-left and occupy roughly the left 52-58% of the frame. The right 36-42% remains clean dark ink-blue negative space for a later media plate; do not place anything important there.

Subject: recognizable Space Shuttle Challenger launch stack with orbiter, external tank, two boosters, launch tower/pad, and paper-model architectural base. The view is a true 3/4 left-side view of the shuttle and pad, with the shuttle's nose/cabin visible and the left side of the orbiter/pad dominant. It is not a side elevation and not a mirrored copy of the reference.

Camera/composition: 1920x1080 horizontal 16:9. Same general premium staging and scale as the attached reference, but newly rendered for the opposite 3/4 side. Challenger should face subtly into the open right side of the frame. Preserve clean title-safe negative space on the right.

Style/material: cascade-paper-architectures-ink-lit-v1, clean low-detail folded paper planes, subtle paper fiber only, no gritty texture, controlled museum-model lighting, cream paper architecture forms, deep ink-blue field, soft lavender paper shadows, premium editorial still.

Avoid: no generated text, logos, labels, smoke, water, explosions, grit, cyan/coral hardware artifacts, or duplicate video card. Also avoid watermarks, UI, captions, phone/media plates, archival footage, horizontal mirror artifacts, copied lighting from the old angle, literal side-profile composition, excessive surface noise, speckles, soot, flame, or atmosphere.

variants.label: variant_02_strict_opposite_camera_selected
variants.prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept.
Asset type: 16:9 YouTube channel trailer source plate, text-free base image.
Input image role: the attached image is composition and Paper Architecture style reference only. Do not edit, trace, reuse, mirror, or copy the attached image.

Primary correction: regenerate the scene from a new camera angle; do not mirror or flip the reference. The attached reference reads as a 3/4 view from one side of the shuttle. Create the opposite 3/4 camera angle as a new rendering: show the shuttle's left-hand side in a 3/4 view, with the orbiter nose/cabin visible and the left side/port side of the orbiter, wing, body, stack, and pad dominant. Physically plausible lighting and shadows for the new angle.

Scene: premium ink-lit Paper Architecture rendering of the Space Shuttle Challenger launch stack and launch pad, made of folded cream paper, vellum, foam-core terraces, scored seams, matte gouache paper, and soft lavender shadows. Challenger/pad sits left/center-left and faces subtly into the open right side of the frame. The right 36-42% of the image is clean deep ink-blue negative space reserved for a later media plate.

Camera/composition: 1920x1080 horizontal 16:9, same general scale, staging quality, dark ink-blue field, and museum-model material language as the attached reference, but newly rendered from the opposite 3/4 side. It must not be a literal side elevation, not a front-on view, and not a mirrored copy. Adjust tower/pad geometry naturally for the new camera angle while keeping the subject left/center-left and not cropped.

Visual style: cascade-paper-architectures-ink-lit-v1, controlled editorial lighting, clean low-detail paper planes, subtle paper fiber only, crisp premium miniature architecture, no procedural-looking textures, no grime.

Strict avoid list: no generated text, logos, labels, smoke, water, explosions, grit, cyan/coral hardware artifacts, or duplicate video card. No captions, UI, phone/media plate, archival footage, watermarks, flame, soot, speckles, rivers, waterfalls, heavy grain, hard stock-photo texture, horizontal mirror artifacts, copied lighting from the old angle, or two shuttle heroes.

short_plate.reason: Source plate must pass left-side 3/4 read before Short plate or motion proof.
